body.question_1: How would you rate the technical competency of the student intern in their relevant field of study?
body.answer_1: The intern's technical competency revealed concerning gaps between what the School of Business Administration's International Business curriculum covers and the practical skills required in a professional workplace environment. While the intern possessed a reasonable theoretical grounding in core concepts, they struggled to translate this knowledge into practical application without significant guidance and hand-holding from senior team members. Proficiency in industry-standard tools and workflows was below the level we typically expect from graduates at a comparable stage, and several foundational technical skills needed to be reinforced on the job. We strongly recommend the School of Business Administration review its curriculum to better align academic content with current industry tool and practice standards.

body.question_2: How well did the intern demonstrate professional communication and interpersonal skills in the workplace?
body.answer_2: The professional attitude demonstrated by this intern from the School of Business Administration's International Business program was superb and genuinely impressed our team throughout the internship period. Punctuality, reliability, and a consistently positive and collaborative work ethic were evident from the very first week, requiring no correction or management intervention to maintain. The intern communicated proactively, raised concerns and questions through appropriate channels, and responded to feedback constructively and without defensiveness. Their professional maturity — particularly in managing competing deadlines, handling ambiguity, and engaging respectfully with colleagues at all levels — reflects a level of preparation that speaks well of the School of Business Administration's commitment to producing work-ready graduates.

body.question_3: How effectively did the intern collaborate with team members and contribute to group projects?
body.answer_3: This intern's teamwork skills were moderate, with a genuine willingness to collaborate balanced by some developmental areas that became apparent as project complexity increased. The intern worked well in low-ambiguity team tasks with clear role definitions, but required additional support when navigating more complex team dynamics involving competing priorities, unclear ownership, or conflicting perspectives. Communication within the team improved noticeably over the internship duration, which is an encouraging sign of learning and adaptability. Further emphasis on collaborative project work, cross-functional team exercises, and conflict resolution skills in the International Business curriculum would produce more team-ready graduates for the professional environment.

body.question_4: How would you assess the intern's problem-solving abilities and approach to challenging or unfamiliar tasks?
body.answer_4: Problem-solving was a inconsistent area for this intern, representing the most significant gap between the School of Business Administration's International Business graduates and our professional workforce expectations. The intern often sought direct guidance from supervisors before attempting independent analysis or solution development, limiting their ability to work autonomously on complex assignments. When initial approaches failed, pivoting strategy independently proved difficult, and the intern sometimes became visibly discouraged rather than treating failure as diagnostic information to inform the next attempt. Building a stronger culture of structured problem-solving — through case studies, debugging exercises, and open-ended challenges — in the International Business curriculum would produce significantly more effective graduates.

body.question_5: How quickly and effectively did the intern adapt to your company's work culture, tools, and environment?
body.answer_5: This intern showed reasonable adaptability to our work environment, adjusting reasonably well to most situations while requiring additional support when changes occurred at a faster pace or scale than anticipated. Day-to-day routine adjustments — such as modified task priorities or new tool adoption — were handled efficiently and without notable disruption. More significant changes, such as project pivots or team restructuring, required more guidance to navigate smoothly and confidently. Greater exposure to agile methodologies, sprint-based workflows, and change management principles in the International Business curriculum would strengthen the adaptability of graduates entering dynamic professional environments.

body.question_6: How proficient was the intern in English communication, both written and verbal, for professional purposes?
body.answer_6: English communication proficiency was a concerning limitation for this intern from the School of Business Administration's International Business program that impacted their effectiveness in several key areas of their role. Written communication — including emails and technical documentation — frequently contained grammatical and structural errors that required editing by supervisors before use in internal or external contexts. Verbal participation in English-language meetings was hesitant, limiting the intern's ability to contribute fully to discussions or to represent the team confidently in broader organizational settings. We strongly recommend the School of Business Administration elevate its English communication training requirements — particularly for professional writing and spoken business English — as a core graduation competency for all programs.

body.question_7: How well did the intern's academic training align with the practical demands and standards of your industry?
body.answer_7: Our curriculum-related feedback for the School of Business Administration's International Business program, based on this internship experience, is reasonable: there is a reasonable foundation but meaningful gaps that the institution could address to significantly improve graduate readiness. Core theoretical knowledge is adequately covered, and the intern was able to apply foundational concepts with reasonable effectiveness. However, gaps exist in applied tool proficiency, project management fundamentals, and the professional communication standards expected in industry settings. We recommend more regular and structured industry consultations to ensure the curriculum remains current, and greater emphasis on workplace simulation through live projects, internships, and industry-sponsored assessments.

body.question_8: What specific technical or soft skill areas do you believe the university curriculum should strengthen for future graduates?
body.answer_8: Our curriculum-related feedback for the School of Business Administration's International Business program, based on this internship experience, is moderate: there is a reasonable foundation but meaningful gaps that the institution could address to significantly improve graduate readiness. Core theoretical knowledge is adequately covered, and the intern was able to apply foundational concepts with reasonable effectiveness. However, gaps exist in applied tool proficiency, project management fundamentals, and the professional communication standards expected in industry settings. We recommend more regular and structured industry consultations to ensure the curriculum remains current, and greater emphasis on workplace simulation through live projects, internships, and industry-sponsored assessments.

body.question_9: How would you rate the intern's overall performance and value contribution to your organization during the placement?
body.answer_9: This intern's overall performance was acceptable, delivering a useful contribution to the team while also clearly indicating areas where additional academic preparation would improve professional readiness. Assigned tasks were generally completed to a functional standard, with quality improving progressively as the intern built familiarity with our tools, processes, and expectations over the placement period. The growth trajectory was positive and suggests genuine potential that a stronger academic foundation would help realize more quickly. We see this internship as a successful learning experience for the student and encourage the School of Business Administration to continue building practical readiness into its curriculum to produce even more effective graduates.

body.question_10: Based on your experience with this intern, would you consider hiring from this university in the future, and why?
body.answer_10: Based on this intern's performance and the overall quality of graduates we have observed from the School of Business Administration's International Business program, we would absolutely consider hiring from this institution in the future — both for internships and for permanent graduate positions. The intern's technical competency, professional attitude, collaborative skills, and growth mindset align closely with the profile we seek when making graduate hiring decisions. We are actively discussing a return internship offer for this individual and have already flagged the School of Business Administration as a preferred graduate source institution for our talent acquisition team. We would encourage the School of Business Administration to continue deepening its industry partnerships and look forward to an ongoing and mutually beneficial relationship with the institution in the years ahead.
